text.data-narration: Factors Affecting Water Availability
Geographical Location: Mountains, hills, and valleys can block or redirect water flow, reducing availability.
Climate Change: Changes in temperature and precipitation patterns affect water availability.
Demands on Water: Agriculture, industry, and urbanization increase water demands.
Types of Water Resources: Surface Water, Groundwater, and Freshwater
text.tspan: Types of Water Resources: Surface Water, Groundwater, and Freshwater

text.data-narration: Surface Water: Rivers, lakes, reservoirs, and wetlands.
Groundwater: Underlying aquifers and wells.
Freshwater: Potable and non-potable water sources.
International and Local Water Resources: International Water Resources are shared by countries across borders, while Local Water Resources are managed at the local level
text.tspan: International and Local Water Resources: International Water Resources are shared by countries across borders, while Local Water Resources are managed at the local level

text.data-narration: International Water Resources: The Nile River is an example of a shared international water resource.
Local Water Resources: Municipal water supply is an example of a local water resource.
Factors Affecting Water Quality: Pollution, Human Activities, and Geological Factors can alter water quality
text.tspan: Factors Affecting Water Quality: Pollution, Human Activities, and Geological Factors can alter water quality

text.data-narration: Pollution: Industrial, agricultural, and domestic waste can contaminate water.
Human Activities: Deforestation, urbanization, and climate change can alter water cycles.
Geological Factors: Rock type, soil composition, and groundwater flow can affect water quality.
Types of Water Pollution: Physical, Chemical, and Biological
text.tspan: Types of Water Pollution: Physical, Chemical, and Biological

text.data-narration: Physical Pollution: Sediment, silt, and suspended solids can contaminate water.
Chemical Pollution: Industrial chemicals and agricultural runoff can contaminate water.
Biological Pollution: Excess nutrients and excess microorganisms can contaminate water.
Conclusion: Water resources are vital for human survival and the environment. We must conserve and manage water resources sustainably
text.tspan: Conclusion: Water resources are vital for human survival and the environment. We must conserve and manage water resources sustainably
